title: Kuehne+Nagel and SWISS Strengthen Partnership to Accelerate More Sustainable Aviation
content: Kuehne+Nagel and Swiss International Air Lines (SWISS) have strengthened their strategic partnership to accelerate sustainable aviation by signing a Memorandum of Understanding focused on innovative technologies. A central component of this collaboration is a shared commitment to synthetic aviation fuel (SAF) produced by Swiss cleantech pioneer Synhelion, which converts sunlight into renewable fuels. To support the scaling of this technology, the companies have entered a long-term offtake agreement starting in 2027. Under this five-year deal, Kuehne+Nagel will purchase Synhelion SAF from SWISS for cargo shipments via Swiss WorldCargo. This agreement provides Synhelion with the necessary planning security to expand production while offering Kuehne+Nagelâ€™s customers a way to reduce their environmental footprint. Executives from both companies highlighted the necessity of such partnerships to make synthetic SAF economically viable and commercially available to meet the aviation industryâ€™s climate targets.
author: Analysts Account
